description: Authoritative evidence table for old vs improved training comparison

evaluator_notes.IS: Importance sampling. Reliable when ESS_median > 1000. Used for old models.
evaluator_notes.MH: Metropolis-Hastings, burn_in=300 or 100, step_scale=0.25. Used for improved models where IS collapsed. Note: MH has inconsistent convergence for models with high accept rate (old model bias); results flagged as uncertain for N=2 comparisons.
evaluator_notes.training_ESS: ESS measured during training (last 20 epochs). Low training ESS indicates training was on nearly degenerate samples.

rows.label: N=4 improved ctnn s305
rows.confidence: LOW - training ESS collapsed, post-training IS ESS=4

rows.label: N=4 improved unified s306
rows.confidence: LOW - training ESS collapsed, post-training IS ESS=9

rows.label: N=2 old ctnn s401 (sep=4.0)
rows.confidence: UNCERTAIN - MH accept rate 0.748 may indicate slow mixing

rows.label: N=2 old ctnn s402 (sep=4.0)
rows.confidence: UNCERTAIN - MH accept rate 0.748 may indicate slow mixing

rows.label: N=2 improved ctnn s301 (sep=4.0)
rows.confidence: LOW - training ESS collapsed; MH accept 0.50 vs IS bias disagreement

rows.label: N=2 old ctnn g6 (sep=6.0)
rows.confidence: HIGH - different physical system from sep=4.0 runs

rows.label: N=2 old unified g7 (sep=6.0)
rows.confidence: HIGH - different physical system from sep=4.0 runs

N4_comparison.confidence: MEDIUM - IS reliable for old, MH for improved
N4_comparison.conclusion: Improved training produces LOWER energy (better variational bound by ~0.75%) but WORSE virial (~13% vs ~3.5-4%). N=4 training-ESS collapsed from 856 to ~22 with improved setup.

N2_comparison.confidence: LOW - MH calibration uncertain for both; training-ESS collapsed for improved
N2_comparison.conclusion: Improved training produces LOWER energy (better by ~1.4%) and apparently better virial (~8.8% vs ~18-19%), but these numbers are unreliable due to training-ESS collapse during improved training.

verdicts.root_cause_of_unreliability: The fd_colloc loss combined with bf_hidden=64 causes training-time ESS collapse: improved N=2 trained on ESS~13/step, improved N=4 on ESS~22/step (vs 1254 and 856 for old setup). This means training gradients were computed from near-degenerate samples, making the resulting wavefunctions unreliable regardless of final-energy values.
verdicts.overall_verdict: The improved training setup achieves lower variational energies (~0.75-1.4% improvement) but causes training-time ESS collapse that makes the wavefunction quality uncertain. The virial comparison cannot be made reliably. The primary bug is in the fd_colloc + large-backflow sampling regime: the proposal is not adapted to the wavefunction during training.
verdicts.recommended_next_step: Fix the training-time sampling for fd_colloc: either add sigma_fs adaptation to track the wavefunction's learned geometry, or switch proposal to a warmup schedule that widens as the backflow learns. Until training-ESS is stable (>500 throughout training), the improved models cannot be trusted.
